title: alcea pallida
intro: Alcea pallida, also known as the narrow-leaved hollyhock or Mexican hollyhock, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, skin conditions, and wounds, due to the presence of anthocyanins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Alcea pallida include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. Alcea pallida can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
